title: Captain Blood
description: Captain Blood — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "Captain Blood" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of Contents'

memes.label: "Admiral"
memes.description: "The senior commander of a naval squadron or fleet; in Captain Blood, the title of admiral marks the highest level of naval authority and is a rank that Blood himself assumes as commander of his buccaneer fleet."

memes.label: Vice-Admiral Craufurd
memes.description: Vice-Admiral of the Royal Navy whose flagship Imperator carried Colonel Bishop and Lord Julian Wade to the Caribbean in pursuit of Captain Blood.

memes.label: "Antilles"
memes.description: "The island chain forming the eastern boundary of the Caribbean Sea, through which Captain Blood's squadron sails and where Spanish, French, and English colonial interests intersect."

memes.label: "Arabella Bishop"
memes.description: "The niece of Colonel Bishop and the novel's heroine — a woman of spirit and intelligence who moves from contempt for the rebel-slave Blood to love for the man she comes to recognize as honourable."

memes.label: "Arabella (ship)"
memes.description: "Peter Blood's flagship — a captured Spanish frigate named in honour of Arabella Bishop, which becomes the most feared vessel in the Caribbean under Blood's command."

memes.label: "Authority"
memes.description: "The legitimate power to command and govern, which the novel repeatedly questions — from the corrupted authority of Judge Jeffreys to the contested authority of colonial governors and Spanish admirals."

memes.label: "Barbados"
memes.description: "The British Caribbean colony where Peter Blood is transported as a slave after the Monmouth Rebellion — the primary setting for the novel's first act and the seat of Colonel Bishop's authority."

memes.label: "Barbados Militia"
memes.description: "The colonial military force of Barbados commanded by Colonel Bishop — representing the armed authority of the plantation system that Blood must overcome to escape."

memes.label: "Battle of Sedgemoor"
memes.description: "The decisive battle of the Monmouth Rebellion, fought on 6 July 1685, in which the forces of King James II defeated the rebel army of the Duke of Monmouth."

memes.label: "Andrew Baynes"
memes.description: "Andrew Baynes, a yeoman arrested alongside Peter Blood at Oglethorpe's Farm, who later became a member of Blood's crew."

memes.label: "Bloody Assizes"
memes.description: "The series of treason trials conducted by Judge Jeffreys in 1685 following the Monmouth Rebellion, resulting in over 300 executions and the transportation of hundreds of rebels, including Peter Blood, to the Caribbean colonies."

memes.label: "Brethren of the Coast"
memes.description: "The loose confederation of buccaneers who roamed the Caribbean Sea in the 17th century — from whose ranks Peter Blood rose to preeminence as the most formidable captain among their number."

memes.label: "Bridgetown"
memes.description: "The principal port and capital of Barbados, serving as the primary setting for Peter Blood's arrival as a convict and the site of the Spanish raid that transforms his fortunes."

memes.label: "Bridgewater"
memes.description: "The Somerset town that serves as the primary setting for the opening of the novel — where Peter Blood arrives in 1685 and is drawn into the Monmouth Rebellion."

memes.label: "Buccaneer"
memes.description: "Caribbean maritime adventurers of the 17th century — originally French hunters on Hispaniola who became sea-raiders — who form the social and military fabric of Tortuga and the world into which Peter Blood is thrust after his escape from slavery."

memes.label: Cahusac
memes.description: A burly, callous Breton buccaneer who served as Blood's lieutenant and later commanded the La Foudre, frequently clashing with Blood over matters of strategy and prize.

memes.label: "Captain Calverley"
memes.description: "A captain in the Royal Navy sent by Colonel Bishop to demand Captain Blood's surrender — only to discover that Blood holds a King's commission and outranks his own authority."

memes.label: Captain Gardner
memes.description: Master of the Jamaica Merchant, the slave ship that transported Peter Blood and the Monmouth rebels to Barbados; a pragmatic seaman who recognized Blood's medical skill and vouched for his physical toughness at the Bridgetown auction.

memes.label: "Captain Hobart"
memes.description: "A dragoon officer of Colonel Kirke's regiment whose encounter with Peter Blood at Oglethorpe's Farm and subsequent testimony at Blood's trial seal the surgeon's fate as a convicted rebel."

memes.label: Capture of the Milagrosa
memes.description: "The naval engagement in which Captain Blood's Arabella captured the Spanish galleon Milagrosa, flagship of Don Miguel de Espinosa, following a masterful boarding action in September 1688."

memes.label: "Caribbean Sea"
memes.description: "The vast tropical sea that forms the principal theatre of Captain Blood's buccaneering campaigns, stretching from the Antilles to the coast of Spanish America."

memes.label: "Carlisle Bay"
memes.description: "The natural harbour of Barbados where Peter Blood and the transported rebels first arrive as slaves, and the waters that become the stage for Blood's escape and later privateer actions."

memes.label: "Cartagena"
memes.description: "The Spanish colonial port city and fortress on the Caribbean coast of South America — target of Blood's most ambitious military operation and the site of his greatest victory."

memes.label: "Cayona"
memes.description: "The principal port and buccaneer stronghold on the island of Tortuga, serving as the buccaneer capital in the Caribbean during the period of Captain Blood."

memes.label: Charles II
memes.description: King of England, Scotland, and Ireland whose death in 1685 triggered the succession crisis that led to the Monmouth Rebellion; referenced in the novel's political and historical context.

memes.label: "Cinco Llagas"
memes.description: "A Spanish warship of twenty guns, originally commanded by Don Diego de Espinosa y Valdez, captured by Peter Blood at Barbados and later rechristened the Arabella — the vessel that would become the most feared pirate flagship in the Caribbean."

memes.label: "Colonel Bishop"
memes.description: "The Deputy Governor of Barbados and Blood's primary antagonist in the first half of the novel — a brutal, grasping plantation owner who purchases Blood as a slave and pursues him with implacable hatred after his escape."

memes.label: "Colonel Kirke"
memes.description: "The commander of the dragoons whose brutal reputation earns his regiment the nickname 'Kirke's Lambs' — his soldiers arrest Peter Blood and other rebels in the aftermath of the Monmouth Rebellion."

memes.label: "Colonial"
memes.description: "Pertaining to the European colonies in the Caribbean; in Captain Blood, the colonial system — with its planters, governors, slaves, and indentured servants — forms the social and economic backdrop against which Blood's rebellion and redemption unfold."

memes.label: "Commission"
memes.description: "A royal warrant authorizing private vessels to act as agents of the Crown — the legal instrument that transforms Peter Blood from proscribed rebel into King's officer, and the central mechanism by which the novel explores the boundary between piracy and legitimate warfare."

memes.label: "Convict"
memes.description: "Political prisoners sentenced to transportation and penal servitude in the Caribbean colonies — the legal condition imposed on Peter Blood and his fellow Monmouth rebels that sets the entire narrative in motion."

memes.label: "Council"
memes.description: "A deliberative assembly of officers convened to make strategic decisions; in Captain Blood, the council of war is the governing mechanism through which the buccaneer fleet makes collective decisions about targets, tactics, and alliances."

memes.label: "Council of War"
memes.description: "The ad-hoc war council formed by Peter Blood aboard the Arabella to make strategic decisions with his senior officers."

memes.label: "Courage"
memes.description: "The quality of facing danger without fear, displayed by Peter Blood and his companions throughout their adventures — from the courtroom defiance to naval combat against superior forces."

memes.label: "Court of St. James"
memes.description: "The royal court of England, which becomes involved in diplomatic disputes over Peter Blood's activities in the Caribbean."

memes.label: "Crown"
memes.description: "The monarchical authority of England, embodied in the king; in Captain Blood, the Crown is the ultimate source of both condemnation and redemption — it is the Crown that sentences Blood to death and the Crown that ultimately pardons and ennobles him."

memes.label: "Curacao"
memes.description: "Dutch colonial settlement in the southern Caribbean, serving as Captain Blood's intended destination after his escape from Barbados and a key navigational waypoint in the novel."

memes.label: "M. d'Ogeron"
memes.description: "The French governor of Tortuga who provides official sanction to the buccaneers of the Brethren of the Coast, transforming their piratical enterprise into a semi-official instrument of French colonial policy — and whose daughter's romantic entanglement with Levasseur creates a dangerous complication for Peter Blood."

memes.label: M. de Cussy
memes.description: French governor of Hispaniola who served as an intermediary between Captain Blood and the French crown, offering Blood a commission in French service.

memes.label: "M. de Rivarol"
memes.description: "A French naval commander (Marquis/Baron) during Blood's service to King Louis XIV — whose vanity, incompetence, and dishonourable conduct represent the corruption of aristocratic authority."

memes.label: Defence of Jamaica
memes.description: "Captain Blood's intervention to save Jamaica and Port Royal from bombardment and pillage by the French fleet under M. de Rivarol, resulting in Blood's appointment as Governor of the colony by Lord Willoughby."

memes.label: "Don Diego de Espinosa"
memes.description: "Spanish admiral and Blood's recurring adversary — a proud and capable commander whose repeated defeats at Blood's hands become a defining arc of the novel's Spanish-English conflict."
aliases: Don Diego de Espinosa y Valdez

memes.label: "Don Diego de Espinosa y Valdez"
memes.description: "Spanish Admiral commanding the Santo Nino and later the Encarnacion — captured by Peter Blood through trickery and compelled to guide the Arabella past Spanish defences, his parole and eventual betrayal form one of the novel's most suspenseful sequences."

memes.label: "Don Esteban"
memes.description: "The son of the Spanish Admiral Don Miguel de Espinosa — captured by Peter Blood aboard the Cinco Llagas and later released, an episode that showcases Blood's strategic blend of courtesy and cunning."

memes.label: "Don Francisco"
memes.description: "The Deputy-Governor of Maracaybo — a slight, nervous, elderly man of high lineage who braves Captain Blood's threats to negotiate a ransom and save his city from destruction."

memes.label: "Don Miguel de Espinosa"
memes.description: "Spanish Admiral of the West Indies and brother to Don Diego — a shrewd and formidable commander who prosecutes the Espinosa family's vendetta against Blood in the later stages of the novel."

memes.label: "Dr. Whacker"
memes.description: "A physician and schemer in Barbados who attempts to recruit Blood into an escape plot — representing the self-interested manipulation that Blood must navigate even among fellow prisoners."

memes.label: Duel with Levasseur
memes.description: "The duel on the sands of Tortuga in which Captain Blood killed the French buccaneer captain Levasseur in single combat, following Levasseur's violation of the buccaneer articles by attempting to withhold a female captive as personal prize."

memes.label: "Duty"
memes.description: "The moral and professional obligation that guides character actions throughout the novel, from Peter Blood's medical duty to wounded men to the military duty of naval officers, frequently in tension with self-interest and survival."

memes.label: "Nicholas Dyke"
memes.description: "A former petty officer in the Royal Navy who became one of Peter Blood's trusted buccaneer crewmen, participating in key actions across the Caribbean."

memes.label: "Elizabeth (ship)"
memes.description: "A twenty-six gun buccaneer vessel in Captain Blood's fleet, captured and rechristened after Queen Elizabeth of England, commanded by Hagthorpe."

memes.label: Escape from Barbados
memes.description: The daring escape of Peter Blood and his fellow convict-slaves from Bridgetown, Barbados, aboard the captured Spanish frigate Cinco Llagas, transforming them from plantation slaves into Caribbean buccaneers.

memes.label: "Execution"
memes.description: "The judicial killing of condemned prisoners, which hangs over the novel's opening chapters as the fate Blood narrowly escapes — replaced by the living death of Caribbean slavery."

memes.label: "Ferguson"
memes.description: "Ferguson, the Duke of Monmouth's chaplain, who preached a treasonous sermon at Castle Field in Bridgewater on the eve of the rebellion."

memes.label: "Fleet"
memes.description: "A group of ships sailing together under unified command; in Captain Blood, the fleet is the measure of naval power in the 17th-century Caribbean, growing from a single captured vessel to a formidable force of five ships."

memes.label: "French West India Company"
memes.description: "The French colonial trading company whose representative, De Cussy, interacts with Peter Blood in the Caribbean."

memes.label: "Front Matter"
memes.description: "Captain Blood: His Odyssey — front matter including title page, table of contents, and publication information."

memes.label: "Galleon"
memes.description: "A large, multi-decked sailing ship used primarily by Spain in the 16th and 17th centuries for warfare and treasure transport; the Spanish galleon is the principal target and prize of Caribbean buccaneers in Captain Blood."

memes.label: "Gentleman"
memes.description: "A social rank and code of conduct in 17th-century England; in Captain Blood, the ideal of the gentleman — combining honor, courtesy, and martial skill — is central to Peter Blood's identity and to the novel's moral universe."

memes.label: "Gibraltar"
memes.description: "Town on the eastern shore of Lake Maracaibo, captured and ransomed by Captain Blood and his buccaneers in a daring raid."

memes.label: "Governor"
memes.description: "The chief administrative officer of a colony, appointed by the Crown; in Captain Blood, the governorship is both a position of absolute local power and the ultimate prize that legitimizes Blood's transformation from outlaw to servant of the Crown."

memes.label: Governor Steed
memes.description: The gout-ridden Governor of Barbados whose authority is repeatedly flouted and then held hostage by Captain Blood, representing the declining power of Stuart colonial administration.

memes.label: "Governorship"
memes.description: "The office and authority of a colonial governor; in Captain Blood, the governorship of Jamaica is the ultimate political prize — the position that transforms Blood from outlaw to Crown official and represents his complete social rehabilitation."

memes.label: Lord Grey
memes.description: A leading figure in the Monmouth Rebellion who purchased his own pardon for forty thousand pounds, contrasting sharply with the fate of the common rebels.

memes.label: "Hagthorpe"
memes.description: "One of Blood's loyal crew members and fellow escapee from slavery — a gentleman who served in the Royal Navy and represents the capable seamen who form the backbone of Blood's buccaneer company."

memes.label: "Hanging"
memes.description: "A method of execution by suspension from a rope; in Captain Blood, hanging is both a judicial punishment and a maritime practice, symbolizing the brutal justice of the 17th-century Caribbean and the ever-present threat of death that drives the novel's action."

memes.label: "Hispaniola"
memes.description: "Large Caribbean island divided between French and Spanish colonial control, serving as a base for buccaneers and a key strategic location in Captain Blood's adventures."

memes.label: "Honour"
memes.description: "The code of gentlemanly conduct that Peter Blood maintains even as a pirate and slave, distinguishing him from common criminals and earning him the respect of enemies and allies alike."

memes.label: "Hostage"
memes.description: "The practice of holding captives for leverage, a recurring mechanism in the novel through which power relations are negotiated between Blood, Spanish authorities, and rival pirates."

memes.label: "Infanta"
memes.description: "A small Spanish ship captured by Captain Blood's buccaneers, the frailer of two prizes taken alongside the San Felipe."

memes.label: "Jamaica"
memes.description: "The largest British colony in the Caribbean and the destination of transported rebels — mentioned as a site of the southern plantations and ultimately the colony Blood governs at the novel's end."

memes.label: "James II"
memes.description: "King of England (1685–1688) whose victory over the Monmouth Rebellion leads to the Bloody Assizes and the transportation of Peter Blood — and whose later commission, offered through Lord Sunderland, offers Blood the path from outlaw to Crown officer."

memes.label: "Jeremy Pitt"
memes.description: "Peter Blood's loyal first mate and fellow escapee from slavery — a young Somersetshire shipmaster who becomes Blood's right hand throughout his career as a buccaneer and privateer."

memes.label: "Judge Jeffreys"
memes.description: "The historical Lord Chief Justice whose 'Bloody Assizes' condemned the Monmouth rebels — appearing in the novel as the personification of corrupted justice and Stuart tyranny."

memes.label: "Jury"
memes.description: "A body of citizens sworn to render a verdict based on evidence presented in court; in Captain Blood, the jury at the Bloody Assizes becomes an instrument of judicial corruption, bullied and coerced into condemning innocent men."

memes.label: "Justice"
memes.description: "The concept of fairness and lawful judgment that is systematically violated by the Stuart regime, creating the moral foundation for Peter Blood's rebellion and eventual vindication."

memes.label: "King William III"
memes.description: "William III of England (William of Orange), who displaced James II in the Glorious Revolution of 1688 — his ascension to the throne reshapes the political landscape within which Peter Blood and his buccaneers negotiate their fate in the novel's final chapters."

memes.label: "La Foudre"
memes.description: "A French ship in Captain Blood's buccaneer fleet, commanded by Cahusac, lost near La Guayra."

memes.label: Last Fight of the Arabella
memes.description: The climactic naval engagement in which Captain Peter Blood's flagship, the Arabella, confronted the Spanish fleet under Admiral Don Miguel de Espinosa — a desperate battle that marked the culmination of Blood's long conflict with Spanish naval forces in the Caribbean.

memes.label: "Letters of Marque"
memes.description: "A government-issued license authorizing a private vessel to attack and capture enemy ships during wartime — the legal instrument that separates privateering from piracy, and the central political tool in Captain Blood's transformation from outlaw to Crown officer."

memes.label: "Levasseur"
memes.description: "A French buccaneer captain and Blood's rival — representing the darker side of piracy, whose brutality and treachery provide a moral counterpoint to Blood's code of honour."

memes.label: List of Concepts
memes.description: Complete index of all concept pages in the Captain Blood wiki, sorted by citation count to surface the most richly sourced articles first.

memes.label: List of Events
memes.description: Complete index of all event pages in the Captain Blood wiki, from naval battles to political trials, sorted alphabetically.

memes.label: List of Persons
memes.description: Complete index of all person pages in the Captain Blood wiki, sorted alphabetically.

memes.label: List of Places
memes.description: Complete index of all place pages in the Captain Blood wiki, from Caribbean colonies to English estates, sorted alphabetically.

memes.label: List of Ships
memes.description: Complete index of all ship pages in the Captain Blood wiki, sorted alphabetically. Includes captured vessels and renamed prizes.

memes.label: Lord Churchill
memes.description: English courtier and military commander whose observation about King James II's character — that his heart was "as insensible as marble" — frames the novel's portrayal of royal ruthlessness.

memes.label: Lord Feversham
memes.description: Royalist commander who led King James II's forces to victory at the Battle of Sedgemoor, defeating the Duke of Monmouth's rebellion; subsequently oversaw the brutal suppression of Monmouth's followers alongside Colonel Kirke.

memes.label: "Lord Gildoy"
memes.description: "A wounded rebel nobleman whose urgent need for medical care draws Peter Blood into the events that lead to his arrest and subsequent transportation to Barbados."

memes.label: "Lord Julian Wade"
memes.description: "An English nobleman sent to the Caribbean to suppress piracy, who becomes Blood's unlikely advocate — recognizing the pirate's honour and ultimately helping to restore Blood to legitimate service."

memes.label: "Lord Willoughby"
memes.description: "King William III's Governor-General of the West Indies — a sharp-tongued, leathery-cheeked colonial administrator whose arrival transforms Peter Blood's fortunes and whose keen eye for merit elevates the former pirate to the governorship of Jamaica."
aliases: King William's Governor-General of the West Indies

memes.label: Lords Commissioners
memes.description: The judicial tribunal of five judges that presided over the Bloody Assizes at Taunton, trying Peter Blood and other Monmouth rebels for treason in September 1685.

memes.label: "Major Mallard"
memes.description: "Commandant of the fort at Port Royal, Jamaica, and a loyal subordinate to Governor Peter Blood during his tenure as governor."

memes.label: "Maracaybo"
memes.description: "Wealthy Spanish colonial city and great freshwater lake on the coast of present-day Venezuela, site of Captain Blood's most famous raid and subsequent naval engagement."

memes.label: "Mary Traill"
memes.description: "Arabella Bishop's young companion who accompanies her during the escape from Barbados and the Spanish raid on Bridgetown."

memes.label: "Mercy"
memes.description: "The quality of showing compassion that becomes the central irony of Peter Blood's fate — condemned for performing an act of mercy, he must navigate a world where mercy is a liability."

memes.label: "Milagrosa"
memes.description: "A Spanish ship captured by Blood — one of the prizes that builds his reputation and wealth in the Caribbean."

memes.label: "Duke of Monmouth"
memes.description: "The 'illustrious and high-born Prince James, Duke of Monmouth' — illegitimate son of Charles II, leader of the failed 1685 rebellion whose defeat at Sedgemoor leads directly to Peter Blood's arrest, trial, and transportation to Barbados, setting the entire novel in motion."

memes.label: "Monmouth Rebellion"
memes.description: "The failed Protestant uprising of 1685 led by James Scott, Duke of Monmouth, against King James II — the historical event whose brutal suppression sets the entire plot of Captain Blood in motion."

memes.label: "Monmouth Rebels"
memes.description: "The rebel army of James Scott, Duke of Monmouth, that attempted to overthrow King James II in 1685; Peter Blood served among its ranks as a physician."

memes.label: "Henry Morgan"
memes.description: "The historical Welsh buccaneer and later Lieutenant Governor of Jamaica — the real-life figure whose exploits form the template against which Peter Blood's career is measured, and the implicit model for the novel's central argument that a pirate may become a governor."

memes.label: "Mr. Pollexfen"
memes.description: "The Judge-Advocate at the Taunton Assizes — a Whig at heart who prosecuted Peter Blood under Judge Jeffreys, yet privately admired the prisoner's courage and wit."

memes.label: "Mrs. Barlow"
memes.description: "Mrs. Barlow, Peter Blood's housekeeper in Bridgewater, who tended his household during the events leading to his arrest."

memes.label: "Mutiny"
memes.description: "Armed rebellion by sailors against their commanding officers, a constant threat in the novel's Caribbean setting where crews of escaped slaves and rebels-convict must be held together by loyalty rather than legal authority."

memes.label: "Navigation"
memes.description: "The art and science of guiding a ship across open water — in Captain Blood, the scarce and critical skill that distinguishes commanders from common sailors, determines the fate of escape attempts, and elevates Jeremy Pitt and Peter Blood above their buccaneering peers."

memes.label: "New World"
memes.description: "The European term for the Americas in the seventeenth century — for Peter Blood, a place of exile that becomes a realm of freedom and opportunity denied him in the Old World."

memes.label: "James Nuttall"
memes.description: "James Nuttall, a shipwright and debtor in Barbados who assisted Peter Blood's escape plot by procuring a boat."

memes.label: "Oath"
memes.description: "A solemn promise, often invoking divine witness, binding the speaker to a course of action or loyalty; in Captain Blood, oaths mark moments of moral commitment, political allegiance, and personal resolve in a world where a man's word is his bond."

memes.label: "Ogle"
memes.description: "A former gunner who becomes one of Peter Blood's most resolute buccaneer commanders, renowned for his gunnery skill and steadfast courage in naval engagements."

memes.label: "Pardon"
memes.description: "An official act of forgiveness that absolves a person of legal consequences for a crime; in Captain Blood, the royal pardon is both a political commodity — bought and sold for fortunes — and the ultimate goal that drives Blood's quest for legitimacy."

memes.label: Peace of Nimeguen
memes.description: The 1678–79 treaties that ended the Franco-Dutch War, marking a chronological pivot in Peter Blood's biography — after the peace, Admiral de Ruyter's movements became "obscure," and Blood's naval service in European waters came to an end.

memes.label: "Peter Blood"
memes.description: "The protagonist of the novel — an Irish physician turned slave, pirate, and eventually Governor of Jamaica. A man of honour, wit, and formidable competence, unjustly condemned for treason after the Monmouth Rebellion."

memes.label: "Petit Goave"
memes.description: "French buccaneer rendezvous port on the coast of Haiti where Captain Blood meets Baron de Rivarol and agrees to join the French assault on Cartagena."

memes.label: "Piracy"
memes.description: "The practice of attacking and robbing ships at sea, which becomes the central mode of survival and rebellion for Peter Blood and his crew after their escape from slavery in 17th-century Caribbean waters."

memes.label: "Plantation"
memes.description: "The agricultural estates of the Caribbean colonies that depend on slave labor and become the destination for transported Monmouth rebels, representing both the economic engine of empire and the site of Blood's degradation."

memes.label: "Plantation System"
memes.description: "The agricultural and economic system of large-scale colonial estates producing sugar, tobacco, and other cash crops through enslaved labour — the engine of Caribbean wealth and the brutal institution into which Peter Blood and his fellow Monmouth rebels are sold as convict labourers."

memes.label: "Port Royal"
memes.description: "The principal English port in Jamaica — known historically as the 'wickedest city on earth' for its pirate economy — appearing in the novel as the seat of English authority and Blood's eventual base as Governor."

memes.label: "Prison"
memes.description: "The condition of confinement that opens the novel — Blood in Bridgewater Gaol — and recurs as both physical reality and psychological metaphor throughout his journey from condemned rebel to colonial governor."

memes.label: "Prisoner"
memes.description: "The condition of captivity — whether as a prisoner of war, a political convict, or a hostage — that recurs throughout Captain Blood as both a narrative device and a moral test, revealing character through how captors treat the captured."

relations.label: Don Diego de Espinosa y Valdez

memes.label: "Privateer"
memes.description: "State-sanctioned maritime raiding — the legal distinction that separates the privateer from the pirate, and the political bridge that eventually transforms Peter Blood from outlaw to Governor of Jamaica."

memes.label: "Privateering"
memes.description: "The practice of state-sanctioned maritime raiding, in which privately owned vessels are licensed by a government to attack enemy shipping — the legal framework that separates the buccaneer from the pirate, and the profession into which Peter Blood is repeatedly invited throughout the novel."

memes.label: Raid on Cartagena
memes.description: "The French naval expedition under Baron de Rivarol, with Captain Blood's buccaneer support, to capture the Spanish colonial city of Cartagena in April 1689, resulting in the city's surrender and pillage."

memes.label: Raid on Maracaybo
memes.description: Captain Blood's buccaneer raid on the wealthy Spanish city of Maracaybo, extracting a ransom of fifty thousand pieces of eight and demonstrating Blood's capacity for large-scale buccaneer operations.

memes.label: "Ransom"
memes.description: "The practice of demanding payment for the release of prisoners, a central economic mechanism of Caribbean piracy in the novel through which Peter Blood funds his operations and negotiates with Spanish authorities."

memes.label: "Rebel"
memes.description: "A participant in armed insurrection against the Crown — the political identity that condemns Peter Blood and his companions to transportation and slavery, and the moral framework through which the novel examines loyalty, treason, and the right of resistance."

memes.label: "Rebellion"
memes.description: "Armed resistance against established authority, which serves as both the novel's historical backdrop (the Monmouth Rebellion of 1685) and the recurring model for Blood's defiance of Spanish and English power in the Caribbean."

memes.label: "Royal Navy"
memes.description: "The naval force of the English Crown, which appears in the novel as both adversary (under James II, hunting Blood as a pirate) and ally (under William III, providing Blood's path to legitimacy)."

memes.label: "San Felipe"
memes.description: "A Spanish vessel captured alongside the Infanta by Captain Blood's buccaneers, damaged during its seizure."

memes.label: "Santo Nino"
memes.description: "A Spanish ship captured by Captain Blood's fleet, intercepted while sailing from La Guayra."

memes.label: "Sedgemoor"
memes.description: "The site of the decisive battle of the Monmouth Rebellion in July 1685, where the rebel army was routed by royal forces — the event that sets Peter Blood's story in motion."

memes.label: "Slavery"
memes.description: "The system of forced labor and penal transportation that shapes the lives of Peter Blood and the rebels-convict in the 17th-century Caribbean colonies following the Monmouth Rebellion."

memes.label: "Somerset"
memes.description: "English county in South West England, home of Peter Blood and setting of the Monmouth Rebellion scenes in Captain Blood."

memes.label: Spanish Admiralty
memes.description: The Spanish naval command in the Caribbean, led by Admiral Don Miguel de Espinosa, tasked with combating piracy and protecting Spanish treasure fleets.

memes.label: Spanish Crown
memes.description: The Spanish monarchy under King Charles II (the "Catholic King"), which claimed dominion over vast territories in the Caribbean and pursued Captain Blood for his acts of piracy against Spanish shipping.

memes.label: "St. Nicholas"
memes.description: "French colonial port on the northwest coast of Hispaniola, a key buccaneer haven where Captain Blood encountered Levasseur and rescued Madeleine d'Ogeron."

memes.label: "Lord Sunderland"
memes.description: "The English Secretary of State under James II, whose letter initiates Lord Julian Wade's mission to recruit Peter Blood for Crown service — the distant architect of the diplomatic manoeuvre that transforms Blood from outlaw into colonial governor."

memes.label: "Supreme Council of Castile"
memes.description: "The highest governing council of the Spanish Empire, which condemned Peter Blood and his buccaneers as pirates."
aliases: Supreme Council of the Catholic King

memes.label: "Tangiers"
memes.description: "North African port city and English garrison where Peter Blood served as a soldier before settling in Somerset."

memes.label: "Tangiers Regiment"
memes.description: "The British army regiment stationed at Tangiers where Peter Blood served before the events of the novel, commanded by Colonel Kirke."

memes.label: "Taunton"
memes.description: "The Somerset town whose maidens wove Monmouth's banners — and where Peter Blood later stands trial in the great hall of Taunton Castle during the Bloody Assizes."

memes.label: "Tobacco"
memes.description: "A prized Caribbean cash crop and trade commodity — from the 'fragrant Sacerdotes tobacco for which Gibraltar was famous' to the humble leaf that fills the pipes of buccaneers and governors alike in the world of Captain Blood."

memes.label: "Tortuga"
memes.description: "The buccaneer stronghold off the coast of Hispaniola that serves as Blood's base of operations — a lawless port where pirates from all nations gather, trade, and plan their next expeditions."

memes.label: "Trade"
memes.description: "The system of maritime commerce, colonial exchange, and mercantile enterprise that underpins the 17th-century Caribbean world of Captain Blood — from the 'trades of peace' practiced by transported rebels to the 'piratical trade' of Tortuga's buccaneers."

memes.label: "Treason"
memes.description: "The crime of disloyalty to the Crown that serves as the legal pretext for Peter Blood's condemnation and transportation, reflecting the political instability of late Stuart England."

memes.label: "Treasure"
memes.description: "Valuable goods, especially gold, silver, and precious cargo, captured at sea or seized from colonial settlements; the economic engine driving Caribbean piracy and privateering in Captain Blood."

memes.label: "Trial"
memes.description: "A formal examination of evidence before a judge and jury to determine guilt or innocence; in Captain Blood, the trial is perverted into a theatrical ritual of condemnation, most notably at the Bloody Assizes where Peter Blood is sentenced to death without meaningful defense."

memes.label: Trinity College Dublin
memes.description: The Irish university where Peter Blood studied medicine before his military and buccaneering careers.

memes.label: "Windward Passage"
memes.description: "Strategic sea passage in the Caribbean between Cuba and Hispaniola, frequently navigated by Captain Blood and other buccaneers."

memes.label: "Wolverstone"
memes.description: "A member of Blood's crew — one of the named buccaneers whose loyalty and competence contribute to Blood's success in the Caribbean."

memes.label: Yberville
memes.description: A young and experienced French sea-wolf who serves as one of Blood's most loyal buccaneer captains, commanding the Lachesis during the later stages of Blood's campaign.
